steps.title: Identify what illustrating the sestina form requires
steps.content: The goal explicitly states “use ONE of the poems” to illustrate. This signals the answer must select a specific poem and demonstrate the form's characteristics using that particular poem's end words.

steps.title: Find the choice that illustrates with one poem correctly
steps.content: Choice B selects “Towards Autumn” specifically and demonstrates the sestina's structure—thirty-nine lines with six end words (daughter, friend, bread, mother, lover, myself) that alternate in a set pattern. The other three fail because A mentions both poems rather than one, while C and D misattribute the end words to the wrong poems. That matches choice B—B is correct.
